Name: Doc-750K
Description: Large-scale document-level multimodal dataset from Sci-Hub, Arxiv, and OpenReview with real QA pairs derived from document structure for training native document understanding models.
Question Types: Abstract writing, Paper titling, Caption writing, Experiment writing, Translation, Document QA
Metrics: Accuracy, F1, ANLS, ROUGE-L, EM, Title/Abstract Edit Distance

Name: DUDE
Description: Multi-page, multi-domain, multi-industry DocVQA benchmark with diverse question types including extractive, abstractive, list, and not-answerable questions.
Domain: Cross-domain (Medical, Legal, Technical, Financial, Government, etc.)
Modalities: Text, Layout, Tables, Lists, Figures, Charts, Maps, Handwriting
Question Types: Extractive, Abstractive, Counting, Arithmetic, Comparison, Layout-navigating, Multi-hop

Name: LongDocURL
Description: Comprehensive benchmark integrating Understanding, Reasoning, and Locating tasks with 20 sub-tasks, covering 396 documents averaging 85.6 pages from CommonCrawl.
Domain: Cross-domain (Research Reports, User Manuals, Books, Theses, Work Summaries, Presentations, Project Proposals, Meeting Minutes)
Metrics: Generalized Accuracy (3-stage protocol: generation, answer extraction, category-specific scoring)

Name: M-LongDoc
Description: Benchmark for super-long multimodal document understanding with open-ended questions requiring in-depth reasoning over documents averaging 210.8 pages.
Question Types: Analytical reasoning, Technical analysis, Domain knowledge, Visual interpretation
Metrics: Correctness score 1-5 via multi-judge (Claude 3.5, GPT-4o, Gemini 1.5 Pro)

Name: M3DocVQA
Description: First open-domain multi-modal, multi-page, multi-document VQA benchmark requiring retrieval across 3,368 PDF documents (Wikipedia) to answer questions.

Name: MM-NIAH
Description: First benchmark for systematically evaluating MLLMs on long multimodal document comprehension via needle-in-a-haystack tasks with text and image needles.
Domain: Synthetic (constructed from OBELICS interleaved image-text data)
Min/Max Pages: N/A (context length: 1K-72K tokens, up to 36 images)
Metrics: Accuracy (per-task and across context-length Ã— needle-depth heatmap)

Name: MMLongBench-Doc
Description: Long-context multimodal document understanding benchmark with 1,082 expert-annotated questions over 135 lengthy PDF documents across 7 document types.
Domain: Cross-domain (Research Reports, Financial Reports, Academic Papers, Brochures, Guidelines, Admin/Industry Files, Tutorials)
Question Types: Factual, Reasoning, Cross-page comprehension, Unanswerable detection
Metrics: Generalized Accuracy, Generalized F1 (ANLS-like rules for short-form answers)

Name: MMVQA
Description: Multipage multimodal information retrieval dataset for PDF-based VQA on research journal articles, retrieving paragraphs, tables, and figures as answers.
Modalities: Text, Layout, Tables, Figures, Figure Captions, Table Captions
Question Types: Content-based (paragraph, table, figure questions across sections)
Metrics: Exact Match (EM), Partial Matching (PM), Multi-Label Recall (MR)

Name: MP-DocVQA
Description: Extension of SDocVQA to multi-page setting; questions posed over documents with 1-20 pages from the UCSF Industry Documents Library.
Modalities: Text, Layout, Tables, Forms, Figures, Handwriting

Name: SlideVQA
Description: Multi-image document VQA dataset of slide decks requiring S-hop, multi-hop, and numerical reasoning, with annotated arithmetic expressions and bounding boxes.
Modalities: Text, Layout, Tables, Figures, Charts, Images, Diagrams
Metrics: Exact Match (EM), F1, Joint Exact Match (JEM), Joint F1 (JF1)

Name: UDA-Benchmark
Description: Benchmark suite for RAG-based real-world document analysis with 6 sub-datasets across finance, academia, and world knowledge domains, using raw unstructured documents.
Metrics: F1 (span-level bag-of-words), EM (with 1% tolerance for FinHybrid), LCS (retrieval)

Name: DuReaderVis
Description: First Chinese open-domain DocVQA dataset with real user queries from Baidu Search and document images from web pages, featuring long answers and multi-span extraction.
Domain: Cross-domain (Open-domain web pages from Baidu Search)
Avg # Pages: N/A (web page screenshots; avg. 1968 OCR tokens per image)
Modalities: Text, Layout, Tables, Lists, Visual features (images, colors)
Answer Types: Extractive (Text, List, Table; S-span and multi-span; avg. 180.54 tokens)
Metrics: Rouge-L, F1 (concatenated spans); Recall@K, Recall@5, Recall@10, MRR (retrieval)

Name: InstructDoc
Description: First large-scale instruction tuning dataset for VDU covering 30 datasets and 12 tasks with diverse human-written instructions in a unified format.
Domain: Cross-domain (Forms, Receipts, Business Docs, Web Pages, Slides, Science Diagrams, UI Screens, Books, etc.)
Modalities: Text, Layout, Tables, Figures, Charts, Icons, Forms, Diagrams
Question Types: KIE, S-page QA, Discrete reasoning, Visual reasoning, Multi-hop reasoning, NLI, Captioning, Classification, Layout analysis
Answer Types: Extractive, Generative, Classification labels, Bounding boxes
Metrics: ANLS (InfoVQA/DUDE/Text-VQA/ST-VQA), EM (SlideVQA), Relaxed Accuracy (ChartQA), entity F1 (FUNSD/CORD), Accuracy (TabFact), ROUGE-L (VisualMRC)

Name: DocVQA
Description: Foundational S-page document VQA dataset on 12,767 industry document images (UCSF tobacco archive) with 50,000 questions, requiring reading text, tables, forms, and layout.
Modalities: Text, Layout, Tables, Forms, Figures, Handwriting

Name: InfographicVQA
Description: VQA on 5,485 infographic images requiring joint reasoning over text, layout, graphics, and data visualizations with elementary arithmetic.
Modalities: Text, Layout, Figures, Maps, Visual elements

Name: ChartQA
Description: Chart-QA benchmark with 9.6K human-written and 23.1K machine-generated questions over 20,882 charts, requiring visual and logical reasoning.

Name: TextVQA
Description: VQA dataset requiring reading scene text in natural images, with 45,336 questions on 28,408 images from Open Images.

Name: VisualMRC
Description: Visual MRC with 30,000+ abstractive Q-A pairs on 10,000+ document images from contemporary webpages requiring reading and reasoning.
Modalities: Text, Layout, Tables, Charts, Visual elements

Name: WikiTableQuestions
Description: Semantic parsing dataset with 22,033 complex compositional questions over 2,108 Wikipedia tables, requiring multi-step logical operations.

Name: TabFact
Description: Large-scale fact verification with 118K human-annotated statements on 16K Wikipedia tables, labeled as ENTAILED or REFUTED.

Name: FeTaQA
Description: Free-form table QA with 10K Wikipedia table-based instances requiring retrieval, reasoning, and generation of elaborate natural-language answers.

Name: QASPER
Description: Information-seeking QA over 1,585 NLP research papers; 5,049 questions written by NLP practitioners from title/abstract, answered with evidence from full text.

Name: Kleister
Description: Key information extraction over long formal documents with complex layouts; two subsets (Kleister Charity UK reports and Kleister NDA US contracts) requiring entity values from multi-page documents.

Name: TAT-DQA
Description: Document VQA over real-world financial reports with both tabular and textual content; 16,558 Q-A pairs over 2,758 document pages requiring discrete reasoning (arithmetic, counting, comparison).

Name: MultiHiertt
Description: Numerical reasoning over multiple hierarchical tables and paragraphs from financial reports; 10,440 Q-A pairs requiring multi-step reasoning across hierarchical table structures.

Name: DocBench
Description: Benchmark for LLM-based document reading systems with 1,102 questions over 229 PDF documents across 5 domains (Academia, Finance, Government, Laws, News), including text-only, multimodal, metadata, and unanswerable question types.

Name: DocGenome
Description: Large-scale scientific document benchmark with 500K arXiv documents from 153 disciplines, annotated with 13 layout attributes and 6 logical entity relationships across 7 tasks (classification, VQA, layout detection, transformation).
Metrics: Accuracy, Edit Distance, mAP, BLEU, F1

Name: DocStruct4M
Description: Structure-aware pretraining set of 4M samples across 5 domains (documents, tables, charts, webpages, natural images) with parsing tasks and multi-grained textâ€“bbox pairs, introduced by mPLUG-DocOwl 1.5.
Domain: Documents, Tables, Charts, Webpages, Natural images
Question Types: Structure-aware parsing, Text grounding, Text recognition

Name: MMLongBench
Description: Comprehensive long-context vision-language benchmark with 13,331 examples across five task categories (Visual RAG, Needle-in-a-Haystack, Many-Shot ICL, Summarization, Long-Document VQA), delivered at five standardized input lengths (8K-128K tokens) via cross-modal tokenization.
Domain: Cross-domain (Wikipedia entities, image albums, classification of cars/food/scenes/species, government & legal reports, long PDFs, slides)
Modalities: Text, Images (interleaved natural + synthetic), PDF documents, slides
Question Types: Visual RAG, NIAH retrieval, NIAH counting, many-shot ICL classification, summarization, long-document VQA
Answer Types: Substring Exact Match (SubEM), Accuracy, Model-based judgment (summaries)

Name: BRIDGE
Description: Multi-hop QA benchmark over long scientific papers requiring evidence aggregation across text, tables, and figures; supports both chain-like and fan-out reasoning structures with explicit multi-hop reasoning annotations enabling step-level evaluation beyond final-answer correctness. Represents a future direction for MP-VRDU benchmarks by emphasising intermediate-evidence grounding.
Domain: Scientific papers (CS/NLP/CV from ACL, EMNLP, CVPR, ICCV; 2023-2025 ArXiv subset)
Question Types: Abstractive (Ab), Comparative (Cp), Causal Reasoning (Re); chain-like and fan-out multi-hop
Metrics: LLM-as-Judge (Audit/Accuracy/Fidelity on 0-5 scale via Qwen-Plus), ROUGE-L, BLEU

Name: PDF-VQA Task A
Description: Page-level document element recognition over scientific articles; verifies element existence and counts elements with relative spatial relationship understanding. Questions and answers cover only a fixed answer space.

Name: PDF-VQA Task B
Description: Page-level document layout structure understanding and key information extraction; questions require understanding spatial layout (relative or reading-order) and recognising specific document elements, with answers as text spans from the page.
Answer Types: Text spans from page (25 document element index classes)

Name: PDF-VQA Task C
Description: Full document-level hierarchical understanding requiring cross-page reasoning; identifies parent or child elements of a queried item (e.g., section that describes a table) with answers extracted from multiple pages.
Question Types: Parent Relationship Understanding, Child Relationship Understanding (cross-page hierarchical)
Answer Types: Text spans from multiple pages (400 document element index classes; multi-answer via Sigmoid > 0.5)
